data.title: Rocket Lab stock (NASDAQ: RKLB) downgraded from Overweight to Sector Weight after 18 months, citing fully priced major growth catalysts. Core catalysts include $816M contract award from Space Development Agency.
data.description: Rocket Lab Corp. (NASDAQ: RKLB) experienced its first stock downgrade in over 18 months as KeyBanc Capital Markets lowered its rating from Overweight to Sector Weight on Thursday. Despite still being recognized as a top-tier company in the space industry, the recent rapid increase in RKLB's stock price has led to this change. The downgrade was influenced by the perception that the market has already factored in the company's significant growth drivers. This includes notable achievements such as securing an $816 million contract from the Space Development Agency in December, effectively doubling its backlog. Rocket Lab's stock is currently on a downward trend, reflecting the impact of this downgrade. For more detailed information, please refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kelun-Biotech presents advancements at 44th JPM Healthcare Conference in San Francisco with Dr. Ge Michael.
data.description: Taking place from January 12 to 15, 2026, the 44th J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference occurred in San Francisco, California, USA. Dr. Ge Michael, serving as the President and CEO of Sichuan Kelun-Biotech Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. ("Kelun-Biotech"), attended the conference.

data.title: Three US lawmakers urge SEC action on Justin Sun case, warning of investor confidence risk from unresolved case against Tron founder.
data.description: Three members of the House of Representatives from the Democratic party expressed concerns that failure to resolve the Securities and Exchange Commission's case against the founder of Tron could potentially damage investors' trust in the regulatory body.

data.title: Save A Lot donates 103,656 bags of food to charities across the US, with the help of customers.
data.description: Save A Lot, a leading discount grocery chain in the US, recently contributed to the fight against hunger by donating a total of 103,656 bags of high-quality food to local charities. The initiative, named Bags for a Brighter Holiday, received immense support from customers nationwide. During the months of October, November, and December, customers purchased pre-assembled food bags to aid their local communities. This effort showcases the impact that collective generosity can have on addressing food insecurity.

data.title: Bitcoin price reversal signals potential $105,000, shorts in trouble above $92,000. Traders warned of bullish move.
data.description: Bitcoin (BTC) has experienced a significant turnaround, with crypto trader DonAlt predicting a potential rally to $105,000. He warns that short sellers are facing challenges as they find themselves stuck above $92,000. DonAlt describes the recent price movement as a classic bear trap, where Bitcoin initially dropped below a key support level but quickly rebounded, a signal he considers highly bullish. The sudden shift caught short sellers off guard, as the price surged back above $92,000, leaving them in precarious positions. DonAlt cautions that those who have shorted Bitcoin are now at risk, as failed breakdowns like this often result in swift upward movements. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Serving Alcohol Inc. approved as first state-approved alcohol training provider in South Carolina, for mandated certification.
data.description: In DELRAY BEACH, Florida, Serving Alcohol Inc., a top provider of responsible alcohol seller-server training, has been approved by the South Carolina Department of Revenue (SCDOR) as the inaugural training provider to aid in the state’s recent alcohol training mandate. South Carolina now necessitates all on-premise alcohol-serving business employees, such as those in bars, to undergo alcohol seller-server training and certification. This move underscores the importance of adhering to regulations aimed at enhancing alcohol service safety.

data.title: FIT introduces Jason S. Schupbach as 7th president, enhancing leadership in creative education.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, in New York, the Fashion Institute of Technology (FIT) introduced Jason S. Schupbach as its seventh president. FIT, renowned for its creative education programs and as a part of the State University of New York (SUNY), is a key player in the sector. Schupbach, a distinguished figure in the realms of art and design, has taken over the helm of the institution.

data.title: Ohio mayors commend Whirlpool Corp's dedication to U.S. manufacturing in opinion piece; strong partnership cited.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, in Benton Harbor, Michigan, an article was released by PRNewswire discussing an opinion piece in The Plain Dealer newspaper in Cleveland, Ohio. The mayors of Clyde and Marion, Ohio praised Whirlpool Corporation for its unwavering dedication to American manufacturing. Whirlpool Corporation, the sole company mentioned, has been recognized for its commitment to supporting domestic production.
